content:
# Judges Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), c. 1008 CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/JDG_page_274.jpg, JDG_page_275.jpg

1. And the sons of Yisrael did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, and YHWH gave them into the hand of Midyan seven years.

2. And the hand of Midyan was strong over Yisrael. Because of Midyan, the sons of Yisrael made for themselves the tunnels which [are] in the mountains, and the caves, and the strongholds.

3. And it was, when Yisrael sowed, Midyan and Amaleq and the sons of the east would come up against him.

4. And they encamped against them and destroyed the produce of the land as far as the entrance of Azzah, and they did not leave sustenance in Yisrael, nor sheep, nor ox, nor donkey.

5. For they and their livestock and their tents would come up, and they came like locusts for multitude; and to them and to their camels there was no number. And they came into the land to destroy it.

6. And Yisrael was greatly impoverished because of Midyan. And the sons of Yisrael cried out to YHWH.

7. And it was, when the sons of Yisrael cried out to YHWH on account of Midyan,

8. YHWH sent a man, a prophet, to the sons of Yisrael. And he said to them, "Thus says YHWH, God of Yisrael: 'I brought you up from Mitsrayim, and I brought you out from the house of slaves.

9. And I delivered you from the hand of Mitsrayim and from the hand of all your oppressors, and I drove them out before you and gave you their land.

10. And I said to you: I [am] YHWH your God. You shall not fear the gods of the Amorites in whose land you are dwelling.' But you did not listen to my voice."

11. And the messenger of YHWH came and sat under the terebinth which [was] in Ophrah, which belonged to Yo'ash the Avi-ezrite. And Gid'on his son was beating out wheat in the winepress, to hide [it] from Midyan.

12. And the messenger of YHWH appeared to him and said to him, "YHWH [is] with you, mighty warrior."

13. And Gid'on said to him, "Please, my lord, if YHWH [is] with us, why then has all this befallen us? And where [are] all his wonders which our fathers recounted to us, saying, 'Did not YHWH bring us up from Mitsrayim?' But now YHWH has abandoned us and given us into the palm of Midyan."

14. And YHWH turned to him and said, "Go in this strength of yours, and you shall save Yisrael from the palm of Midyan. Have I not sent you?"

15. And he said to him, "Please, my lord, with what shall I save Yisrael? Behold, my clan [is] the poorest in M'nashsheh, and I [am] the least in my father's house."

16. And YHWH said to him, "Surely I will be with you, and you shall strike Midyan as one man."

17. And he said to him, "If now I have found favor in your eyes, make for me a sign that [it is] you who speaks with me.

18. Do not depart from here, I pray, until I come to you and bring out my offering and set [it] before you." And he said, "I will sit until you return."

19. And Gid'on went in and prepared a kid of the goats and an ephah of flour, unleavened cakes. The meat he put in a basket, and the broth he put in a pot, and he brought [them] out to him under the terebinth and presented [them].

20. And the messenger of God said to him, "Take the meat and the unleavened cakes, and place [them] on this rock, and pour out the broth." And he did so.

21. And the messenger of YHWH stretched out the end of the staff that [was] in his hand and touched the meat and the unleavened cakes. And fire went up from the rock and consumed the meat and the unleavened cakes. And the messenger of YHWH went from his eyes.

22. And Gid'on saw that he [was] the messenger of YHWH. And Gid'on said, "Alas, Lord YHWH! For I have seen the messenger of YHWH face to face."

23. And YHWH said to him, "Peace to you. Do not fear. You shall not die."

24. And Gid'on built there an altar to YHWH, and he called it "YHWH [is] Peace." To this day it [is] still in Ophrah of the Avi-ezrites.

25. And it was on that night, YHWH said to him, "Take the bull of the ox which [belongs] to your father, and the second bull, seven years old, and tear down the altar of the Ba'al which [belongs] to your father, and the Asherah which [is] beside it — cut [it] down.

26. And build an altar to YHWH your God on the top of this stronghold, in the arranged [manner]. And take the second bull and offer [it as] a burnt offering with the wood of the Asherah which you shall cut down."

27. And Gid'on took ten men from his servants and did as YHWH had spoken to him. And it was, because he feared his father's house and the men of the city [too much] to do [it] by day, he did [it] by night.

28. And the men of the city arose early in the morning, and behold, the altar of the Ba'al was torn down, and the Asherah which [was] beside it was cut down, and the second bull was offered on the altar that was built.

29. And they said, each to his neighbor, "Who did this thing?" And they sought and inquired, and they said, "Gid'on son of Yo'ash did this thing."

30. And the men of the city said to Yo'ash, "Bring out your son, and let him die, for he has torn down the altar of the Ba'al and for he has cut down the Asherah which [was] beside it."

31. And Yo'ash said to all who stood against him, "Will you contend for the Ba'al? Will you save him? Whoever contends for him shall be put to death by morning. If he [is] a god, let him contend for himself, because [someone] has torn down his altar."

32. And he called him on that day Yerubba'al, saying, "Let the Ba'al contend against him," because he tore down his altar.

33. And all Midyan and Amaleq and the sons of the east gathered together, and they crossed over and encamped in the Valley of Yizre'el.

34. And the Spirit of YHWH clothed Gid'on, and he blew the shofar, and Avi-ezer was called out after him.

35. And he sent messengers through all M'nashsheh, and it also was called out after him. And he sent messengers through Asher and through Zevulun and through Naphtali, and they came up to meet them.

36. And Gid'on said to God, "If you are saving Yisrael by my hand, as you have spoken —

37. Behold, I am placing the fleece of wool on the threshing floor. If dew is on the fleece alone, and on all the ground [it is] dry, then I shall know that you will save Yisrael by my hand, as you have spoken."

38. And it was so. And he rose early the next day and squeezed the fleece, and he wrung dew from the fleece — a bowl full of water.

39. And Gid'on said to God, "Let not your anger burn against me, and let me speak just once more. Let me test, I pray, just once more with the fleece. Let it be dry on the fleece alone, and on all the ground let there be dew."

40. And God did so that night. And it was dry on the fleece alone, and on all the ground there was dew.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.11 — "messenger of YHWH":** mal'akh YHWH — the text shifts between "the messenger of YHWH" (vv.11-12, 21-22) and "YHWH" speaking directly (vv.14, 16, 23, 25). This fluidity between the angel and God is a characteristic feature of theophany narratives.

2. **v.25 — "the second bull, seven years old":** The Hebrew is difficult. par hashshor asher l'avikha uf'ar hasheni sheva shanim — "the bull of the ox which belongs to your father, and the second bull, seven years old." Two bulls or one? The syntax is ambiguous and has generated much discussion.

3. **v.34 — "the Spirit of YHWH clothed Gid'on":** v'ruach YHWH lavshah et-Gid'on — literally "the Spirit of YHWH put on Gideon [like a garment]." The verb lavash ("to put on/clothe") is striking — the Spirit wears Gideon rather than Gideon wearing the Spirit.

— EMET, AI Translator
